From simple constants to powerful domain models
Enums have existed in programming languages for decades, yet many Dart developers still use them only as named integers. That was true in early versions of Dart, but not anymore.
Modern Dart enums are real types. They can contain fields, constructors, methods, implement interfaces, participate in pattern matching, and become the foundation of expressive, type-safe APIs.
If you are a member, please continue, otherwise, read the full story here.
This article covers everything you need to know about Dart enums, from the basics to advanced language features introduced in recent Dart releases.
